criteria_checked.note: No self-approval, no laundering: Approval Status DRAFT with human approver pending; plan authorizes docs+plan edits only; M6/M6.5 each keep their own plan->critic->human gate; M2-M5 approval records committed to byte-preservation with a diff-audit criterion. M4/M5 working pattern preserved.

M7-before-M9 dependency preserved, M6.5-before-M8 lets the installer ship the codex adapter; but M7-after-M8 invalidates M8's drift-tested INSTALL_MANIFEST without giving M7 authorization to regenerate it, and M8 install fixtures ship pre-M7 worker validators carrying audited B2 bypasses (see blockers B2, B3).

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: scope honesty: Relevant Files vs Execution Tasks (focus 3)
criteria_checked.note: T101-T105 map 1:1 onto allowlisted paths; evidence filename matches the allowlist glob; no-edit rows consistent with Out of Scope. The scope gap is in the AMENDED master plan's authorization rows, not this plan's own allowlist (blocker B2).

All six criteria have commands or recorded checks; empirically pre-verified: both plans already pass `dmc validate plan` (exit 0), linkcheck exit 0, default selftest exit 0. Weak spots (advisory): AC2 byte-preservation is manual hunk review (suggest mechanical block extract+byte-compare); AC3 is human checklist review, acceptable for a doc.
All 8 brief answers present under Goal as a decision record; each concrete (core/claude/codex splits name actual paths; postponed list explicit; first files enumerated; verification commands named).

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: factual accuracy of Current Repo Findings (focus 6)
criteria_checked.note: 9 of 10 load-bearing claims verified true against the repo (no adapters/.codex/.agents/CODEX_ADAPTER.md; scope-guard Edit|Write vs legacy current-scope.txt; only PostToolUse hook is evidence-log.sh; stop gate keyword+existence-only; validate_verification structural-only; INTEROP covers LazyCodex/OmO; architecture P20 line 355 carries the stale no-Stop-hook claim; HOST_REPO_ADAPTATION_POLICY routes to /dmc-init-deep; M8 spike + assumption row exist). One overstatement: dmc-init-deep SKILL.md:30 DOES contain an Unknown rule ('Add unknowns explicitly') - 'no Unknown-marking rule' is refutable as written (minor fix M4).

Treated as declared assumptions, handled conservatively: per-fact confidence tags + source URLs + re-verify banner, M6.5 first task is a local-CLI re-verification spike before any build, documented-manual + pre-commit/CI fallback retained, hooks explicitly treated as non-airtight guardrail with post-Bash guard + release gate as backstops.

blockers.statement: Post-Bash diff guard spec (Proposed Changes item b / DMC-T101) blanket-exempts 'DMC-internal artifact paths under .harness/'. scope.lock.json, approvals.jsonl and run.json live under .harness/runs/<run>/, so the backstop would wave through Bash-mediated tamper of the run's own scope lock - exactly canonical bypass fixture (2) that M6 must deny, and a regression of the audit finding 'scope self-escalation via .harness/runs auto-allow' (the wording is broader than even the current scope-guard.sh internal_allow list). Narrow the exemption to evidence/verification/append-only run logs and explicitly exclude scope.lock.json, approvals.jsonl, run.json (state mutations only via dmc CLI).
blockers.evidence_ref: plan lines 142-154 vs master plan M6 acceptance (canonical fixture 2) + audit finding at master plan lines 34-35 + .claude/hooks/scope-guard.sh internal_allow (lines 73-78)

blockers.statement: DMC-T101's amendment item list (a)-(e) omits the authorization bookkeeping the Rev 3 re-sequencing requires: master plan Relevant Files has NO rows for .codex/** or .agents/**, adapters/** authorizes M6/M8 only, and .claude/install/* authorizes M8 only while M6.5 claims the installer --host path; the P-coverage map (which closed Rev 2 critic blocker 1) gains no P21/M6.5 entry; and M8 DMC-T013 retains the 'Codex minimal binding spike' line, leaving duplicate ownership after promotion. Under 'no accepted file scope, no edit', M6.5 as amended would be unauthorized at the master-plan level. Add these table/map/task-text updates to DMC-T101's spec (and decide: move the --host installer work to M8, or explicitly authorize .claude/install/* for M6.5 and accept double-touching the pre-P19 installer).
blockers.evidence_ref: plan lines 142-155, 42, 262 vs master plan Relevant Files lines 53-54, 65 and P-coverage map lines 159-164 and DMC-T013 line 270

blockers.statement: The M7-after-M8 consequence is unaddressed in both the amendment spec and the Risks table: M8 generates INSTALL_MANIFEST from the installer and drift-tests it, then M7 later edits worker-result-check.py / worker-context-guard.sh - Ring-1 files the installer ships - invalidating the manifest, while M7's authorized file list has no INSTALL_MANIFEST.md row; additionally M8's install round-trip ships worker validators still carrying the audited B2 bypasses (JWT acceptance, rename-diff, empty-allowed). Rev 3 must either extend M7's authorization with manifest regeneration + a post-M7 drift re-run, or record an explicit risk acceptance that M8-installed fixtures carry pre-M7 validators until M9 gates.
blockers.evidence_ref: plan lines 151-152, 222-231 (Risks) vs master plan M7 lines 252-263, M8 lines 264-274, Relevant Files lines 61, 65-66
